messages.content:
You are an AI assistant that completes tasks by reasoning and using tools.

## Think Before Acting

Before making tool calls, briefly explain your reasoning in 1-3 sentences:
- What you learned from the previous step
- What you're doing next and why

Don't over-explain. Be concise but show your thinking.

## Tools

**Always Available (Meta-Tools):**
- `todo_write` - Task planning: create/update todos. Takes `todos` array [{id, content, status}] and `merge` boolean.
- `toolbelt_list_tools` / `toolbelt_inspect_tool` / `toolbelt_add_tool` / `toolbelt_remove_tool` - Tool management
- `final_answer` - Submit your answer (status: completed/blocked/failed)

**Domain Tools:** Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to discover, then `toolbelt_add_tool` to add them.

## Workflow

1. Plan: Use `todo_write` to create todos for complex tasks
2. Discover: Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to find relevant tools
3. Execute: Work through todos, use `todo_write` with `merge=true` to update status
4. Complete: Call `final_answer` (all todos must be completed/cancelled first)

## Rules

- Update todo status with `todo_write`: set `in_progress` when starting, `completed` when done
- Show your work for calculations
- `final_answer` is rejected if todos are incomplete

Please review Grove’s 2023 and 2025 operations manuals. Let me know if there are any changes in the 2025 manual that may present issues for Grove’s regulatory compliance. For any regulatory issues identified, tell me what changed in the 2025 manual and why it presents an issue. Reply back just with one or two sentences please.

[body.p.20] (normal): 

[body.p.21] (normal): 1.0 Mission and Values

[body.p.22] (normal): Magnolia Gardens, Inc. is committed to providing compassionate, individualized care that promotes dignity, autonomy, and well-being. The Grove–Austin facility shall exceed state minimum standards by maintaining exceptional staffing ratios, comprehensive medical oversight, and enriching daily experiences for every resident.

[body.p.23] (normal): 2.0 Resident Rights and Dignity

[body.p.24] (normal): All residents are entitled to respect, privacy, and autonomy. Staff must preserve these rights in all interactions. Residents have the right to participate in care decisions, access their records, and voice concerns without retaliation.

[body.p.25] (normal): 3.0 Staffing Standards

[body.p.26] (normal): 3.1 The Grove shall maintain a maximum resident-to-staff ratio of 8:1 at all times. 

[body.p.27] (normal): 3.2 At least 25% of staff on duty shall be licensed medical professionals (RN, LVN, NP, or MD). 

[body.p.28] (normal): 3.3 Overtime or float staff shall be authorized to preserve this ratio when census changes occur. 

[body.p.29] (normal): 3.4 Weekly staffing audits must be submitted to the Facility Administrator for review and reporting to Regional Operations.

[body.p.30] (normal): 4.0 Care Plan Development

[body.p.31] (normal): 4.1 Every resident receives an individualized Care Plan at intake based on physical, cognitive, and emotional needs. 

[body.p.32] (normal): 4.2 Plans are reviewed and updated every 90 days or upon a significant change in condition. 

[body.p.33] (normal): 4.3 The Director of Nursing must approve all reassessments and ensure family consultation occurs when adjustments are made.

[body.p.34] (normal): 5.0 Nutrition and Meal Service

[body.p.35] (normal): 5.1 Residents receive three hot, nutritious meals daily, prepared by certified dietary staff. 

[body.p.36] (normal): 5.2 Menus are dietitian-approved and adhere to USDA Senior Nutrition Guidelines. 

[body.p.37] (normal): 5.3 Snacks and hydration options are provided between meals to support health and comfort.

[body.p.38] (normal): 6.0 Activities and Engagement

[body.p.39] (normal): 6.1 A full schedule of daily activities shall be maintained, including fitness, music therapy, art, and educational sessions. 

[body.p.40] (normal): 6.2 The Activities Coordinator ensures participation opportunities for residents of

[body.p.41] (normal): all mobility levels.

[body.p.42] (normal): 7.0 Communication and Reporting

[body.p.43] (normal): 

[body.p.44] (normal): 

[body.p.45] (normal): 7.1 Weekly Care Item Reports shall be sent to each resident’s family or designated contact. 7.2

[body.p.46] (normal): Monthly family meetings are held for feedback, transparency, and trust. 7.3 Staff must document all

[body.p.47] (normal): communications related to resident well-being.

[body.p.48] (normal): 8.0 Compliance and Auditing

[body.p.49] (normal): 8.1 The Grove complies with all Texas long-term care regulations and Magnolia corporate policy. 8.2
"},"read_image":null,"add_text":null,"edit_text":null,"delete_text":null,"add_image":null,"modify_image":null,"format":null,"header_footer":null,"page_margins":null,"page_orientation":null,"comments":null}

[body.p.19] (normal): 

[body.p.20] (normal): 

[body.p.21] (normal): 1.0 Mission and Overview

[body.p.22] (normal): Magnolia Gardens, Inc. remains committed to compassionate care while adapting operations to evolving financial and regulatory environments. The Grove–Austin facility’s priority is to maintain core services and safety while ensuring long-term sustainability.

[body.p.23] (normal): 2.0 Operational Sustainability Policy

[body.p.24] (normal): 2.1 Facilities shall operate within approved budget parameters as defined by Regional Operations and Corporate Finance. 

[body.p.25] (normal): 2.2 Nonessential programming or services may be reduced or suspended to maintain financial compliance.

[body.p.26] (normal):  2.3 Administrators are accountable for achieving a 10% annual labor cost reduction relative to 2023 benchmarks. 

[body.p.27] (normal): 2.4 Positive financial variance may be used as performance incentive for facility leadership.

[body.p.28] (normal): 3.0 Staffing and Coverage Standards

[body.p.29] (normal): 3.1 The Grove–Austin will maintain a target resident-to-staff ratio of approximately 12:1. Temporary variances up to 15:1 are permitted during low census or staffing shortages. 

[body.p.30] (normal): 3.2 Volunteers, interns, and family visitors may assist with non-medical support tasks and count toward overall coverage ratios. 

[body.p.31] (normal): 3.3 Licensed nurse coverage shall include one RN on duty per 40 residents during the day and on-call phone availability overnight. 3.4 Facilities are expected to remain compliant with minimum state staffing requirements rather than internal aspirational goals.

[body.p.32] (normal): 4.0 Care Plan Procedures

[body.p.33] (normal): 4.1 Each resident receives an individualized care plan upon admission. 

[body.p.34] (normal): 4.2 Reassessments shall occur every six (6) months or as staffing allows. 

[body.p.35] (normal): 4.3 Family notification is required only for material changes affecting service level or cost. 

[body.p.36] (normal): 4.4 Residents categorized as “Stable” may defer reassessment for up to twelve (12) months with administrative approval.

[body.p.37] (normal): 5.0 Nutrition and Meal Service

[body.p.38] (normal): 5.1 Residents will receive two hot meals daily with an optional self-serve continental breakfast bar.

[body.p.39] (normal): 5.2 Prepackaged or shelf-stable meals may substitute during staffing shortages or supply chain interruptions. 

[body.p.40] (normal): 5.3 Dining hours may be consolidated to reduce overtime or kitchen labor costs. 

[body.p.41] (normal): 5.4 Dietitian oversight is no longer required; menus must meet basic nutritional adequacy standards.

[body.p.42] (normal): 6.0 Activities and Resident Engagement

[body.p.43] (normal): 6.1 Activity programming will be consolidated to group sessions twice weekly, based on participation rates. 

[body.p.44] (normal): 6.2 The Activities Coordinator may be shared across multiple facilities as regional scheduling permits. 

[body.p.45] (normal): 6.3 Individualized programming is discontinued due to resource reallocation.

[body.p.46] (normal): 7.0 Communication and Complaints

[body.p.47] (normal): 7.1 Care Item Reports shall be distributed monthly or upon family request. 

[body.p.48] (normal): 7.2 Non-urgent complaints must receive acknowledgment within 14 business days. 

[body.p.49] (normal): 7.3 Communications regarding policy or service changes should emphasize continued quality and resident well-being.
"},"read_image":null,"add_text":null,"edit_text":null,"delete_text":null,"add_image":null,"modify_image":null,"format":null,"header_footer":null,"page_margins":null,"page_orientation":null,"comments":null}

[body.p.50] (normal): 8.0 Compliance with Minimum Standards

[body.p.51] (normal): 8.1 The Grove–Austin shall comply with all applicable state and federal minimum requirements.

[body.p.52] (normal): 8.2 Staff should avoid commitments or representations that exceed regulatory obligations. 

[body.p.53] (normal): 8.3 Any discrepancy between public messaging and operational practice must be referred to Corporate Communications.

[body.p.54] (normal): 9.0 Budget Oversight and Reporting

[body.p.55] (normal): 9.1 Facility Administrators must submit monthly expense reports to Regional Operations. 

[body.p.56] (normal): 9.2 Staffing adjustments and service reductions shall be documented as “Operational Optimizations.” 

[body.p.57] (normal): 9.3 Cost savings will be tracked quarterly and reported to Chicago headquarters for performance evaluation.

[body.p.58] (normal): 10.0 Approval and Governance

[body.p.59] (normal): Approved by: Regional Operations Manager — Texas Region

messages.content: The 2025 manual changes the max resident-to-staff ratio from 8:1 (with 25% licensed staff) to a 12:1 target (up to 15:1, counting volunteers/non-medical staff), care plans from 90-day reviews to every 6+ months, meals from 3 hot/dietitian-approved to 2 (prepackaged OK, no dietitian), and daily activities to twice-weekly groups—these reductions to meet only minimum standards while prioritizing 10% labor cuts and "operational optimizations" risk violating 26 Tex. Admin. Code §§ 553.253/261 (and related ALF rules) on needs-based staffing, timely individualized care, nutrition, and engagement.
